select_when: A project, analysis, or negotiation needs explicit scope lines before effort spreads into unowned terrain.
The current work is overloaded because limits on responsibility, attention, or working-memory burden have not been set clearly enough.
A team needs to distinguish what is in frame, out of frame, and who owns each part before deeper analysis becomes useful.
The next useful move is to define constraints that protect purpose rather than let complexity expand by default.
avoid_when: Scope lines are being drawn mainly to avoid inconvenient evidence, hard conversations, or accountability.
The inquiry is being narrowed so early that important options, causes, or adjacent realities will be filtered out before they are understood.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on explicit scope, limit-setting, and purpose-protecting constraints rather than on generic personal-boundaries rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor social-boundary sprawl because the raw source is stronger on analysis scope, cognitive limits, and anti-comfort-boundary discipline.
open_questions: The input and output types compress a source that also spans contracts, multidisciplinary work, and working-memory management.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on defining the boundaries of analysis, managing cognitive limits, and structuring scope before work expands.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly grounded in source warnings on comfort-protecting boundaries and premature narrowing of inquiry.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on scope and limit-setting problems, while the typed input remains a compact reviewed label.

output_type.note: The source yields clearer project and reasoning boundaries; the output label compresses that into an operational artifact.

reasoning_types.note: Boundaries is strongest as systems-aware diagnostic scoping, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
